summary: The City of San Diego's Economic Development Department is seeking proposals for a comprehensive Software as a Service (SaaS) Grants Management System (GMS) to streamline and enhance the department's ability to manage and track local and federal grants and funding programs, including Community Development Block Grant (CDBG), Emergency Solutions Grant (ESG), HOME Investment Partnership Program, Business Improvement Districts, and Capacity Building programs. The solution must provide end-to-end grant lifecycle management including application intake with multi-phase review workflows, document management, financial and billing functions with SAP integration, reporting and compliance capabilities aligned with HUD federal regulations, and a public-facing portal for external grant recipients to submit applications, execute agreements, and manage reimbursement requests. The contract is structured as a three-year base period with two one-year extension options (not to exceed five years total), with a desired go-live date of July 1, 2026, and pricing must be firm, fixed, and fully burdened. The system must integrate with ArcGIS, IDIS, SAP, OKTA, and MS365, comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and Section 508 requirements, maintain 99.9% uptime availability, and support training for up to 75 internal staff, 10 system administrators, and 200 external nonprofit professionals. This procurement supports the City's broader digital transformation goals by replacing legacy tools including SharePoint, SeamlessDocs, and Salesforce-backed systems with a unified platform that ensures federal compliance, operational efficiency, and improved service delivery to approximately 20-30 competitively awarded agreements and 30 Business District contracts annually.
periodOfPerformance: 3-year base period plus two 1-year option periods (5 years maximum)
title: Request for Proposal (RFP) for Grants Management System
